rust_dependencies_used.name: serde_json
rust_dependencies_used.note: default-features = false; preserve_order is INTENTIONALLY NOT enabled because the canonicalization scheme requires sorted-key Map<String, Value> iteration

covered_behaviors: produces deterministic output across two runs
does not import, shell out to, or depend on Python runtime

makefile_targets_added.reason: WORK ORDER 012 §required_makefile_targets — do not add to make ci yet

known_limitations: Independence is logical (separate language, no Python imports, derived from spec) but not organizational — the crate ships in the same repository under the same first-party owner. This does NOT constitute third-party validation per IMPLEMENTATION_TRACKER.md §1.

Canonicalization parity is exercised against the 10 committed corpus entries; the corpus does not include every JCS edge case (e.g., very small/large floats requiring shortest-roundtrip, non-BMP characters interacting with UTF-16 surrogate-pair ordering, control-char escapes inside string values). Out-of-corpus inputs MAY diverge from the Python verifier; if observed, that divergence is a Rust-vs-Python canonicalization gap to be filed and fixed, not a v0.1.0 protocol issue.
The CLI reads from a hard-coded repo-root layout (parent of CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR). It does not accept a --repo-root flag yet.

Number serialization relies on serde_json's default Display for serde_json::Number, which uses Ryu / shortest-roundtrip; this matches Python repr for every float in the current corpus but is not exhaustively cross-validated.
The Rust verifier is not yet wired into make ci, per WORK ORDER 012 prohibition. Promotion is a separate work order.
largest_remaining_external_validation_ga: True third-party validation: a verifier authored, owned, and operated by a non-first-party party (i.e., not Wise.Est Systems). The Rust track established here demonstrates that the spec is implementable in a second language from a different module surface, but it does not change ownership. See IMPLEMENTATION_TRACKER.md §1 and §6.
next_code_task: Either (a) promote the Rust verifier into make ci once stability is observed across at least one more cold run, or (b) seed an actual third-party implementation track (TypeScript or Go) under an independent repository owned by a non-first-party party, per IMPLEMENTATION_TRACKER.md §2.{2,3}.
